module.name: agent
module.purpose: Agent crate namespace for model/session/task/prompt/step/execution orchestration and the plan hub module. Step owns task-step routing, emits plan events, and builds the execution-analysis follow-up plan when an execution completes; PlanHub routes plan events and runs plans by reserving step-number blocks and emitting StepEvent::Trigger; the execution module's ExecutionHub tracks tool executions keyed by execution signature, accumulates their output, and emits StepEvent::Complete back to the step layer on completion; and TaskState now owns that ExecutionHub instead of an execution-signature-to-step map.

childModules.name: execution
childModules.purpose: Owns agent-side execution tracking: Execution values keyed by ExecutionSignature that accumulate ordered output and finalize on the expected update count, and an ExecutionHub holding a Mutex<HashMap<ExecutionSignature, Execution>> that spawns execution steps, routes Update/Succeed events, and emits StepEvent::Complete to the step layer on completion while reporting per-signature status.

childModules.name: model
childModules.purpose: Owns agent model backend traits, request/response data, backend errors, and the implemented Deepseek streaming backend. Internal backend implementations expose request directly instead of a send adapter.

childModules.name: plan
childModules.purpose: Owns PlanHub, PlanRecord, PlanStringify, and PlanError for insertion-ordered task plan storage, plan-event routing, step-block reservation, lookup, completion tracking, and current/pending plan text rendering for prompts.

childModules.name: prompt
childModules.purpose: Defines the agent prompt interface, render_template rendering, InitialPrompt, and ExecutionAnalysisPrompt for execution-output analysis.

childModules.name: session
childModules.purpose: Owns Session startup/routing behavior, forwarding Task status/preview and Step events to clients through a matches! filter and routing task, step, execution, and plan events back to the owning Task worker, plus SessionContext snapshots for prompts.

childModules.name: step
childModules.purpose: Owns the top-level agent runtime Step type re-exported by lib.rs; Step holds Arc<TaskState> and owns task-step routing (StepEvent::Trigger/Complete), model-prompt rendering, plan-event emission, step/model completion handling, and building the execution-analysis plan (PlanEvent::Trigger) when an execution completes; execution step spawning is delegated to the execution hub.

childModules.name: task
childModules.purpose: Owns agent task runtime and TaskState (session context, plan_hub, an ExecutionHub owning execution tracking, a monotonic step_count allocator, and the step queue); step routing lives on Step, plan events route to PlanHub, and execution spawning/routing lives in the execution hub.
